narrative: The RBNZ’s first rate hike in three years dominates the weekend tape, setting up NZD for outperformance at the Tokyo open. NZD/USD closed Friday at 0.5763; the hawkish pivot likely sustains the NZD 1W avg lead of +1.331% against the laggard CHF (Friday close: 0.8078 for USD/CHF). While Iran-driven oil volatility is likely to support CAD at the open, upcoming US CPI and Warsh testimony would likely provide a geopolitical risk premium for USD, pressuring EUR/USD from its Friday close of 1.1419.
